channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: 'Hormuz Control Is A Must': Iran Withdraws From US Deal Over New Blockade

media:content.media:description: The US first imposed the blockade in mid-April and then lifted it in mid-June

item.description: Days of retaliatory strikes across the Middle East by Iran — and both nations' attempts to vie for control of the waterway through which a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas passes during peacetime — threaten to push the region back to all-out war.
item.content:encoded: Days of retaliatory strikes across the Middle East by Iran — and both nations' attempts to vie for control of the waterway through which a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas passes during peacetime — threaten to push the region back to all-out war.

item.title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Restores Iran Port Blockade Amid Rising Strait Of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

item.title: "Tremendous Chemistry": Trump Welcomes New Iraqi PM During White House Visit

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump gave Iraq's new prime minister an effusive welcome at the White House on Tuesday.

item.description: President Donald Trump gave Iraq's new prime minister an effusive welcome at the White House on Tuesday, promoting the "tremendous chemistry" between him and a fellow wealthy businessman.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump gave Iraq's new prime minister an effusive welcome at the White House on Tuesday, promoting the "tremendous chemistry" between him and a fellow wealthy businessman.

item.title: Ex-Columnist Receives $5.63 Million Owed By Trump In Sex Abuse case

media:content.media:description: It is the first time Trump has been forced to pay Carroll.

item.description: The writer E. Jean Carroll has collected nearly $5.63 million from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.
item.content:encoded: The writer E. Jean Carroll has collected nearly $5.63 million from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

item.title: Trump Threatens To Hit Iran Power Plants, Bridges Next Week If No Deal

media:content.media:description: Asked how long the US strikes would carry on, Trump replied: "They'll continue until I say it's enough."

item.description: President Donald Trump told Fox News on Tuesday he would expand US strikes on Iran next week to target power plants and bridges if Tehran does not make a deal.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump told Fox News on Tuesday he would expand US strikes on Iran next week to target power plants and bridges if Tehran does not make a deal.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 4,734, More Than 16,700 Injured

media:content.media:description: The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes struck within a minute of each other on June 24.

item.description: The death toll from the earthquakes that struck Venezuela almost three weeks ago has surpassed 4,700, while almost 17,000 people have been injured, an official report released Tuesday stated.
item.content:encoded: The death toll from the earthquakes that struck Venezuela almost three weeks ago has surpassed 4,700, while almost 17,000 people have been injured, an official report released Tuesday stated.

item.title: UK To Announce Midnight Social Media Curfew For Teenagers

media:content.media:description: Britain will introduce a default overnight curfew on. social media apps for 16- and 17-year-olds.

item.description: Britain will introduce a default overnight curfew on social media apps for 16- and 17-year-olds, the government said, adding new restrictions for older teenagers to its plan to introduce a sweeping ban on social media for under-16s.
item.content:encoded: Britain will introduce a default overnight curfew on social media apps for 16- and 17-year-olds, the government said, adding new restrictions for older teenagers to its plan to introduce a sweeping ban on social media for under-16s.

item.title: Florida Man Killed After Fleeing US Immigration Agents, 3rd Death In A Week

media:content.media:description: It was the third death in about a week involving encounters with ICE agents.

item.description: A man running from an encounter with immigration and other federal agents in Florida was struck and killed by a tractor trailer on Tuesday, authorities said.
item.content:encoded: A man running from an encounter with immigration and other federal agents in Florida was struck and killed by a tractor trailer on Tuesday, authorities said.

item.title: US Forces Launch New Round Of Strikes On Iran

media:content.media:description: US forces carried out strikes against Iran for a fourth day in a row on Tuesday.

item.description: The United States launched strikes against Iran for the fourth day in a row Tuesday as Washington prepared to reimpose a naval blockade on Iranian ports, the US military said.
item.content:encoded: The United States launched strikes against Iran for the fourth day in a row Tuesday as Washington prepared to reimpose a naval blockade on Iranian ports, the US military said.

item.title: US Resumes Hormuz Blockade Against Iran, Launches New Strikes

media:content.media:description: The US carried out another wave of strikes ahead of reimposing the blockade on Tuesday.

item.description: Iran War: The US military said Tuesday that it has reimposed its blockade of Iranian ports in response to Iran's attacks on commercial ships on the Strait of Hormuz, as the interim ceasefire deal unravels and concerns grow about a return to all-out war.
item.content:encoded: Iran War: The US military said Tuesday that it has reimposed its blockade of Iranian ports in response to Iran's attacks on commercial ships on the Strait of Hormuz, as the interim ceasefire deal unravels and concerns grow about a return to all-out war.

item.title: US Halts Immigration Traffic Stops After 2 Fatal Shootings

media:content.media:description: Last week, an ICE agent shot dead Lorenzo Salgado, a 52-year-old Mexican, during an operation in Texas.

item.description: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement has suspended the use of traffic stops to detain immigrants after two fatal shootings by ICE agents, according to media reports Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement has suspended the use of traffic stops to detain immigrants after two fatal shootings by ICE agents, according to media reports Tuesday.

item.title: Trump's Thursday Speech To Focus On "Free And Fair Elections"

media:content.media:description: Republican Trump has long pushed false allegations that he won the 2020 election.

item.description: US President Donald Trump said he would discuss "free and fair" voting in an address to the nation on Thursday, apparently doubling down on his false claims to have won the 2020 election.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump said he would discuss "free and fair" voting in an address to the nation on Thursday, apparently doubling down on his false claims to have won the 2020 election.

item.title: Virgin Atlantic 'Flight To Nowhere' Circles For Hours Before Returning To London
item.description: Virgin Atlantic confirmed to the media outlet the turnaround was due to a "minor technical issue".
item.content:encoded: Virgin Atlantic confirmed to the media outlet the turnaround was due to a "minor technical issue".

item.title: After Hormuz, Iran Eyes Choking Off Red Sea As Trump Card Against US

media:content.media:description: The war, launched in late February by the US and Israel, has destabilised the Gulf.

item.description: As US strikes deepen inside Iran and Houthi attacks escalate in tandem, analysts say Tehran is widening the conflict and seeking to increase pressure on Washington by extending the threat to global trade and energy supplies beyond the Gulf.
item.content:encoded: As US strikes deepen inside Iran and Houthi attacks escalate in tandem, analysts say Tehran is widening the conflict and seeking to increase pressure on Washington by extending the threat to global trade and energy supplies beyond the Gulf.

item.title: Meta Used AI To Target Workers With Medical Conditions For Layoffs: Lawsuit

media:content.media:description: A Meta spokesperson on Tuesday said the claims lack merit.

item.description: Meta laid off 10 per cent of its global workforce in May, or nearly 8,000 people, and was planning more job cuts later this year.
item.content:encoded: Meta laid off 10 per cent of its global workforce in May, or nearly 8,000 people, and was planning more job cuts later this year.

item.title: Warren Buffett Drops Gates Foundation From Donation Plan Over Epstein Links

media:content.media:description: Buffett stepped down as Berkshire CEO in January after 60 years of leading the company.

item.description: Gates denies any knowledge of Epstein's crimes and has not been accused of any wrongdoing. He said he only met with Epstein because he thought it might help him raise money for charitable causes.
item.content:encoded: Gates denies any knowledge of Epstein's crimes and has not been accused of any wrongdoing. He said he only met with Epstein because he thought it might help him raise money for charitable causes.

item.title: Houthis Claim Downing Of Saudi Drone Amid Biggest Clash Since 2022 Truce

media:content.media:description: This comes a day after the Houthis accused Saudi of attacking rebel-held Sanaa airport.

item.description: For more than a decade, aircraft entering Yemeni airspace have needed prior clearance from the Saudi-led coalition that backs the government and says it enforces the restriction at its request.
item.content:encoded: For more than a decade, aircraft entering Yemeni airspace have needed prior clearance from the Saudi-led coalition that backs the government and says it enforces the restriction at its request.

item.title: Netanyahu Warns Iran Of "Much More Powerful" Strikes If Attacked Again

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu's threat came amid new US strikes on Iran.

item.description: "I will say it to the leaders of Iran: Do not count on things remaining quiet if you attack us," Netanyahu said at a conference in Dimona.
item.content:encoded: "I will say it to the leaders of Iran: Do not count on things remaining quiet if you attack us," Netanyahu said at a conference in Dimona.

item.title: Ukraine's Prime Minister Resigns As Zelensky Orders Government Reshuffle

media:content.media:description: Ukraine's parliament formally approved Svyrydenko's resignation in a vote on Tuesday.

item.description: The Ukrainian leader has yet to name a replacement and has said only that Kyiv is "changing its political strategy" amid "new challenges and new tasks".
item.content:encoded: The Ukrainian leader has yet to name a replacement and has said only that Kyiv is "changing its political strategy" amid "new challenges and new tasks".

item.title: Trump Backs Russia Sanctions Bill Posing 500% Tariff Threat To India: Report

media:content.media:description: The proposal was introduced by late Republican Senator Lindsey Graham

item.description: The situation became more complicated after a temporary US Treasury waiver expired on June 17, 2026. The waiver had allowed India's purchase of Russian crude without attracting sanctions. Its expiry has left India's in a legal grey zone.
item.content:encoded: The situation became more complicated after a temporary US Treasury waiver expired on June 17, 2026. The waiver had allowed India's purchase of Russian crude without attracting sanctions. Its expiry has left India's in a legal grey zone.
